categories.label: Foundations
categories.subline: Who you are and who you serve
categories.description: The bedrock the rest of your marketing stands on. Here you will find your brand DNA, your product positioning, the research on who your customers really are, and the competitor landscape — the source-of-truth documents every page, ad and email is built from.

categories.label: Messaging & Copy
categories.description: Your words, ready to use. The approved copy for every page of your site, your shortlist of taglines and hooks, and the core messages that keep everything sounding like one clear, confident voice.

categories.label: Website & Conversion
categories.description: Everything that shapes the site itself and how well it turns visitors into enquiries. Your sitemap and design system, your pricing and plans, the sign-up journey, your free AI assessment tool, and the page-by-page plan for lifting conversions.

categories.label: Demand & Growth
categories.subline: Getting found and bringing people in
categories.description: How new people discover you and decide to get in touch. Your ready-to-run Google and Meta ad campaigns, the free resources that earn email sign-ups, and the step-by-step plan for launching to the right audiences.

categories.label: Lifecycle & Retention
categories.description: What happens after someone puts their hand up. The automated emails that follow up with new leads, and the welcome flow that gets every new customer off to a strong, well-looked-after start.

categories.label: Build & Technical
categories.description: The technical side of how the site gets built. The developer handoff spec, the pre-build checklist, and the action plan behind how everything came together — handy for your developer and for seeing exactly what was asked for.

assets.teaser: The master record of who you are.
assets.summary: The single source of truth for who Fast Web Guru is — your positioning, your story, your voice, your values, and the rules that keep everything on-brand. Every other document in here draws from this one. Open it whenever you want to remind the team (human or AI) exactly what your business stands for.

assets.teaser: What you sell, who it's for, and why it wins.
assets.summary: How we describe what you sell, who it's for, and why it beats the alternatives. Includes your audience and personas, the objections buyers raise (and how to answer them), the competitor landscape, and the exact words your customers use. This is the reference for any new page, ad, or pitch.

assets.teaser: The people you serve, in their own words.
assets.summary: A deep look at the people you serve — what they want, what frustrates them, and the exact language they use to describe both. The raw material behind every headline and offer. Read it before writing anything aimed at a customer.

assets.teaser: Who you're up against, and where the gaps are.
assets.summary: Profiles of the businesses you're up against — what they offer, how they talk, and where they leave gaps you can own. Use it to sharpen your positioning and to answer 'how are you different?' with confidence.

assets.teaser: How Fast Web Guru should always sound.
assets.summary: Worked examples of how Fast Web Guru should sound — the words to use, the words to avoid, and good-versus-bad rewrites. Hand this to anyone (or any AI) writing on your behalf so everything sounds like you.

assets.teaser: Positioning for your Cortex AI add-on.
assets.summary: The positioning and audience work specifically for Cortex — your three-pillar AI add-on. Covers who it's for, the pitch for each pillar, and the objections to handle. The reference for selling Cortex into your existing base.

assets.teaser: The copy for every page of your site.
assets.summary: The current, approved copy for every page of your website — headlines, body, and calls-to-action, page by page. This is what goes live. Open it when you want to review or tweak the words on any page.

assets.teaser: The earlier integrated copy draft.
assets.summary: An earlier integrated draft of the full-site copy, kept for reference. Superseded by Your Website Words, but useful to see how the messaging evolved.

assets.teaser: Your shortlist of taglines, scored and ranked.
assets.summary: A scored shortlist of taglines across four angles — speed, AI access, partnership, and simplicity — with notes on where to use each. Pull from here whenever you need a line for an ad, a bio, or the top of a page.

assets.teaser: The handful of ideas every page should land.
assets.summary: The handful of core ideas your marketing should land again and again — your message pillars. Keep everything you publish anchored to these so the brand says one clear thing, not ten fuzzy ones.

assets.teaser: Where visitors drop off, and how to fix it.
assets.summary: A page-by-page review of where visitors drop off and what to change to turn more of them into enquiries — headlines, calls-to-action, social proof, and the tests worth running. Open this before tweaking any key page.

assets.teaser: Conversion plan for the Cortex add-on.
assets.summary: The conversion plan specifically for selling Cortex — the two buyer funnels, the barriers on each page, and the changes and tests to lift sign-ups. Use it when optimising the Cortex pages.

assets.teaser: Every page and how they connect.
assets.summary: The blueprint of your website — every page, how they're organised, and how visitors move between them. Open it when you're planning a new page or rethinking your navigation.

assets.teaser: Your colours, fonts, and visual rules.
assets.summary: Your colours, fonts, spacing, and the visual rules that keep the site looking sharp and consistent. The reference for anyone designing a page, a graphic, or a new section.

assets.teaser: The path from interested to signed up.
assets.summary: The step-by-step path a new customer takes to sign up — what they see, what they fill in, and where to remove friction. Open it when you want more of your interested visitors to actually get started.

assets.teaser: The free tool that captures your leads.
assets.summary: The design for your 'Instant AI Assessment' — the free tool that gives visitors tailored suggestions and captures them as leads. This is your front door for new enquiries; open it to refine how it works.

assets.teaser: What you charge and how it's packaged.
assets.summary: How your plans are priced and packaged, and the thinking behind it — what each tier includes and why. Open it before you change a price or add a plan.

assets.teaser: Ready-to-run Google and Meta ad copy.
assets.summary: Ready-to-run Google and Facebook/Instagram ad copy, organised by audience and campaign, with the angles, headlines, and landing pages for each. Hand it straight to whoever runs your ads.

assets.teaser: Free resources that earn email sign-ups.
assets.summary: The plan for free resources that earn email sign-ups — what to give away, who it's for, and how it feeds your follow-up. Open it when you want to grow your list with the right people.

assets.teaser: How to put your new site and offer live.
assets.summary: The step-by-step plan for putting your new site and offer in front of people — what to do before, during, and after launch. Open it when you're ready to make some noise.

assets.teaser: The automated emails that nurture new leads.
assets.summary: The automated email sequence that follows up with new leads — what each email says, when it sends, and what it's trying to achieve. This is your salesperson that never sleeps.

assets.teaser: How new customers get up and running.
assets.summary: How a new customer gets up and running in their first days — the steps, the messages, and the moments that make them feel looked after. Open it to make sure every new client gets off to a strong start.

assets.teaser: The technical build spec for your site.
assets.summary: The technical specification handed to whoever builds the site — pages, components, and exactly what changes where. Mostly for your developer, but here so you can see what was asked for.

assets.teaser: Everything to settle before coding starts.
assets.summary: The checklist of everything that needs to be settled before a single line of code is written — content, assets, decisions. A handy reference to make sure nothing was missed.

assets.teaser: The working plan behind your website build.
assets.summary: The working plan behind the website build — priorities, sequence, and the decisions made along the way. A behind-the-scenes look at how your site came together.
